violations.text: Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Equipment was turned off, and remaining ice was voluntarily disposed of during the time of inspection. Establishment is to clean, sanitize and allow equipment to air dry prior to returning unit to service. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/06/2025

The door to the outside, located in the receiving area in the basement, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 12/06/2025

The pre-flush spray hose at the dishwasher is hanging below the flood rim. A plumbing system shall be installed to prevent backflow of a liquid or gas contaminant into the water supply by providing an air gap or installing an approved backflow prevention device. New Violation. Correct By: 12/06/2025

The flooring material in the dishwashing and steamer drain area was observed splitting or beginning to curl and is no longer durable, easily cleanable and nonabsorbent. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 05/06/2026
The hand sink faucet in the south wing kitchenette area was observed leaking and is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 11/20/2025

1-Door Reach-In #2 was observed held at an ambient temperature of 45 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food items had been in unit for less than 4 hours and were moved to other refrigeration. Establishment shall not store TCS food items in unit until an ambient temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or less is obtained. Service call was placed during inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 10/23/2024

The receiving door to the outside, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 11/06/2024

The cutting boards in the kitchen are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 11/06/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the air circulating fan and the refrigerator door gaskets have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned at a frequency necessary to prevent the accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 10/23/2024
The faucet at the 3-bay sink was observed to be leaking and in poor repair. Plumbing systems shall be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 10/30/2024

Standing water on the floor was observed next to cook line by the 3-bay sink and by the dishwasher. Sewage and other liquid waste are to drain into a proper drainage system or be cleaned at a frequency necessary to prevent the accumulation of waste. PIC directed staff to squeegee liquid waste into floor drain and then mop area. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/23/2024
